content.full_text:
Padre Dam Municipal
Water District

10887 Woodside Avenue / P.O. Box 719003
Santee, CA 92072-9003
Telephone: 619-448-3111
FAX Administration: 619-449-9469
FAX Operations: 619-449-9537

Board of Directors:
Douglas S. Wilson
Division 1
Francis G. Daugherty
Division 2
W.E."Baron" Klostermann
Division 3
Wilma R. Carroll
Division 4
G.L. "Jerry" Johnson
Division 5

February 6, 1991

Mr. John Wallace
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95805

RE: Request for Advanced Ruling -
(1) Revised water rate structure and (2) mandatory water
conservation ordinance.

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I refer you to my prior letters of February 9 and February 28,
1990, letters concerning my status as a Director of Padre Dam
Municipal Water District and Vice President in charge of 2500 acres
real estate master plan development located in the same district.
Your response was dated March 16, 1990.

My status has not changed since then as I was re-elected
(unopposed) in November, 1990 and the development is still in the
planning/review stage with the separately incorporated City of
Santee. As before, this project has no vested rights and as yet
has received no approvals other than adopted criteria establishing
goals for it's ultimate development.

At issue is the current water crisis facing the entire state. Two
separate and distinct actions face Padre Dam in this regard; water
rate structure and mandatory water conservation.

Water Rate Structure:
The main water purveyor to southern California known as "METRO" has
implemented an "Incentive"/"dis-incentive" rate structure program
aimed at water conservation. Specifically, METRO is charging 3
times the normal water rate (per acre foot) to water districts that
use beyond "base level" water usage established as 85% of 1989/90
actual usage. Further, as an incentive to use even less the base
level quantities, METRO will reduce normal rates by 1 time for
those districts which beat the base level target.

I have been assigned to a two member sub-committee for Padre Dam
MWD to work with staff for purposes of recommending an equitable
(as possible) new water rate formula for our water customers. The
attorney for our water district - Mr. Wally Peck of Jennings,
Engstrand & Henrikson indicated he felt I have no conflict in
participating fully on this effort.

[illegible]

[The majority of this document appears to be heavily faded or illegible typewritten text. The image quality makes it extremely difficult to accurately transcribe the content with certainty. There are scattered words and phrases visible but they are fragmented and often unclear, making a reliable complete transcription impossible without risking significant errors.]

February 7, 1991
Mr. John Wallace
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2

Mandatory Water Conservation:

Padre Dam MWD currently has a mandatory water conservation
ordinance broken into four stages with stage 1 being "voluntary"
and stages 2-4 being compulsory. Revisions to this ordinance are
anticipated due to the experience gained in last summers stage 2
enactment in response to County Water Authority's (CWA) 10%
reduction goal.

Amongst others, the current ordinance indicates restrictive use
provisions for "construction waters" which are used in conjunction
with construction projects to grade and compact soils, etc. Much
debate surrounds this issue as existing citizens are being asked to
conserve water in the face of new developments.

May I participate and vote on a revised ordinance which includes
issues of growth restriction? If not, may I separate the growth
related items from the remaining items (i.e., water odd/even days,
etc.) and actively participate in those? I would like to re-state
here that the matter planned community is currently raw land which
uses no water (other than landscaping along a roadway we currently
maintain) and has no entitlement or map approvals. Although perhaps
not relevant, I generally favor restricting growth (on an equitable
basis) but believe that only regional (perhaps state-wide) growth
controls will have any measurable effect on our state-wide water
crisis.

Our board will be considering this item in February/March and I
would very much appreciate your advice as soon as humbly possible.
Unfortunately, METRO implemented their new rate structure with
hardly any notice and all the water districts are scrambling to
revise their rate structures now even though METRO's new
incentive/dis-incentive program went into effect February 1.

Please respond to the address indicated below.

MR. DOUGLAS S. WILSON
NEWLAND CALIFORNIA
9225 CARLTON HILLS BLVD., #23
SANTEE, CA    92071

Respectfully,

Douglas S. Wilson

[This image appears to be a faded or low-quality scan of a typed document. The text is largely illegible due to the poor image quality, fading, and what appears to be reversed or inverted printing. While I can make out that this is a multi-paragraph document with what seems to be formal or official content, I cannot reliably transcribe the specific words with confidence. The document appears to have headers or sections, but the text throughout is too unclear to transcribe accurately without extensive speculation.]
